title: Smart Thermo-Electrical Lunchbox
overview_text: A portable smart lunchbox design featuring an automated heating system. I developed the electronics and control logic to ensure safe, energy-efficient food warming.
problem_statement: The need for a portable, lightweight, and affordable solution to actively heat meals to a safe serving temperature (70°C) without the bulk of traditional heaters.
objectives: Heat 500g of food to 70°C in under 30 minutes.
Automated temperature regulation with safety interlocks.
engineering_process: Thermal load calculation, electronics component selection, Arduino-based logic programming, and prototype testing for temperature ramp-up.
technical_details: 60W PTC Heater; Arduino Uno; 20mm Polyurethane insulation; 2mm Stainless Steel liner.
design_decisions: Integrated a self-regulating PTC heater to improve inherent safety against thermal runaway.
challenges: Balancing heating power requirements with device weight limits.
results: Prototype achieved 70°C in 16 minutes with stable thermal cut-off control.
key_learning: Gained experience in cross-disciplinary integration of electrical loads and mechanical insulation.
